name: out-of-our-minds-the-power-of-being-creative
description: Ken Robinson's passionate argument that creativity is as important as literacy — and how education systems systematically suppress it. Explores divergent thinking, multiple intelligences, and fostering creativity.
useCase: Understand why creativity is suppressed by education and culture, and how to reclaim and foster creative capacity in yourself, schools, and organizations.
knownRisks: Strong critique of mainstream education may upset some
Some may find the arguments too focused on Western education
Solutions are more philosophical than prescriptive
